original: Taylor Swiftconcert in Seattle shakes the ground so hard it registers as equivalent to a magnitude 2.3 earthquake and does the same the following night
Marvel superhero film "Avengers: Endgame" becomes the world's highest-grossing film, overtaking "Avatar" and earning $2.9 billion
Hottest day on Earth: average global temperature of 17.15°C (62.87°F) surpasses record set the previous day, according to EU's Copernicus Climate Change Service
Wiley Postcompletes the first solo flight around the world, taking 7 days and 19 hours
"One Piece," world's best-selling manga and comic series, written and illustrated by Eiichiro Oda, first appears serialized in "Weekly Shōnen Jump" in Japan
India's lunar mission, Chandrayaan-2 successfully takes off from Satish Dhawan Space Center, Sriharikota aiming to be fourth nation to soft-land on the moon
S. E. Hinton [Susan Eloise Hinton], American pioneering YA author "The Outsiders", born in Tulsa, Oklahoma
TV show "Supergirl" announces casting of first live-action transgender superhero, by transgender actor Nicole Maines as Nia Nal
South African golfer Branden Grace records the lowest round for a men's major championship with a 62 in the 3rd round at the British Open, Royal Birkdale, England
Alexander Mackenziereaches the Pacific Ocean, becoming the first European to complete a transcontinental crossing of Canada
Crew of Belgian RV Belgica sees first sunrise in 1,600 hours during its first expedition to endure Antarctic winter
Jane Bolin becomes the 1st African American female judge in New York
William Archibald Spooner, English Oxford Don and reverend and inventor (spoonerisms), born in London, England (d. 1930)
Lightning kills 504 sheep in Utah's Wasatch National Park
Oldest Qur'an fragments are discovered in the collection of Birmingham University, with radiocarbon testing dating them to AD 568 - AD 645
Diamonds found in Minas Geras, Brazil
Cleveland, Ohio, founded by General Moses Cleaveland. Originally called 'Cleaveland', the public adopted the current name after a newspaper editor noticed the name was too long to fit on the page.
Ed Wood's cult classic "Plan 9 from Outer Space," often called one of the worst films ever, premieres starring Gregory Walcott andBela Lugosi
Sobhuza II, Paramount Chief and Ngwenyama of Swaziland (1899-1982) who holds the record for the longest reign of any monarch in recorded history, born in Zombodze, Swaziland (d. 1982)
Katharine Lee Bates writes "America the Beautiful" in Colorado
Caterina Jarboro sings "Aida" at the New York Hippodrome, becoming the first Black female opera singer to perform in the United States
Edward Hopper, American painter (Nighthawks, House by the Railroad), born in Upper Nyack, New York (d. 1967)
David Spade, American comedian and actor (SNL; Tommy Boy; Black Sheep; : Just Shoot Me!), born in Birmingham, Michigan
Don Henley, American rock drummer, singer, and songwriter (Eagles - "Desperado"; "Hotel California"; solo - "Boys Of Summer"), born in Linden, Texas
MLB Atlanta Braves pitcherGreg Madduxthrows a complete game with just 78 pitches in 4-1 wi n over the Cubs at Wrigley Field in Chicago
Washington Senators future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherWalter Johnsonbecomes 1st to reach 3,000 career strikeout milestone (en route to 3,508) with 5 K's in 3-1 win over Cleveland Indians
Hashim Amla smashes 311 not out in South Africa's inning and 12 run 1st Test win over England at The Oval; first South African to score a triple-century in Test cricket
William Sigei runs world record 10k (26:52.53)
1st US Venus probe, Mariner 1, fails at lift-off
Venera 8 makes soft landing on Venus
USSR launches Sputnik 50 & Molniya 1-12 communications satellite
Soyuz TM-3 launches with three cosmonauts (one Syrian)
Space shuttle STS-70 (Discovery 20) lands
Soyuz TM-15 launches
Soyuz TM-17 lands
23rd & last part of Comet Shoemaker-Levy hit Jupiter (since July 16th)
French submarine Minerve rediscovered off coast of Toulon, France, after disappearing in 1968 with loss of 52 crew
Hungarian army under John Hunyadi defeats the forces of OttomanSultan Mehmed IIand ends the Siege of Belgrade (Nándorfehérvár), halting Ottoman expansion in Europe for 70 years
US forces led by GeneralGeorge S. Pattonliberate Palermo, Sicily
Duke of Wellingtondefeats French at Battle of Salamanca, Spain
In their second clashSonny Listononce again KOsFloyd Pattersonin round 1 at the Convention Center, Las Vegas to retain the world heavyweight boxing title
Droupadi Murmu is the first tribal politician to be elected President of India (head of state, without executive powers)
Janusz Korczak [Henryk Goldszmit], Polish-Jewish educator and pedagogue (ran an orphanage in the Warsaw Ghetto) [or born in 1879], born in Warsaw, Congress Poland (d. 1942)
John Francis Burke, American surgeon and co-inventor of artificial skin, born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 2011)
H. Boyd Woodruff, American microbiologist whose research led to antibiotics, born in Bridgeton, New Jersey (d. 2017)
Friedrich Bessel, German astronomer and mathematician (star parallax, Bessel functions), born in Minden, Kingdom of Prussia (d. 1846)
Gustav Hertz, German physicist (Nobel Prize for Physics 1925 for "discovery of the laws governing the impact of an electron upon an atom"), born in Hamburg, Germany (d. 1975)
Benjamin Hanby, American composer ("Jolly Old Saint Nicholas"), born in Rushville, Ohio (d. 1867)
George Clinton, American funk-rock singer, songwriter, producer and bandleader (Parliament-Funkadelic), born in Kannapolis, North Carolina
Emily Saliers, American folk-rock singer-songwriter (Indigo Girls - "Closer to Fine"), born in New Haven, Connecticut
Will Calhoun, American jazz and rock drummer (Living Colour - "Love Rears Its Ugly Head"), born in the Bronx, New York City
Tim Brown, American Pro and College Football Hall of Fame wide receiver (Heisman Trophy 1987, Notre Dame; 9X Pro Bowl, LA/Oakland Raiders), born in Dallas, Texas
Scott Dixon, New Zealand auto racer (IndyCar Drivers' champion 2003, 08, 13, 15, 18), born in Brisbane, Australia
Seth Fisher, American comic book artist and penciller (Fantastic Four/Iron Man: Big in Japan), born in Seattle, Washington (d. 2006)
Tablo [Daniel Lee], Korean-Canadian hip-hop musician (Epik High), born in Seoul, South Korea
Otto Waalkes, German comedian, comic book artist and voice actor (Ice Age), born in Emden, Germany
Slick Watts, American basketball point guard (NBA All-Defensive First Team, NBA steals & assists leader 1976 Seattle SuperSonics), born in Rolling Fork, Mississippi (d. 2025)
Ezekiel Elliott, American NFL running back (Dallas Cowboys), born in Alton, Illinois
Marília Mendonça, Brazilian Sertaneja singer-songwriter, born in Cristianópolis, Brazil (d. 2021)
Federico Valverde, Uruguayan football player (Real Madrid, Uruguay), born in Montevideo, Uruguay
